In five years, AI will drive the cost of labor down to zero. Your rent will be free, groceries will be free, electricity will be free. Of course, the subscription fee to access the oxygen required to enjoy these things will be $4,999 a month, billed quarterly.

We are about to enter a post-scarcity utopia. There will be no more work, no more stress, and no more income. You will sit in your zero-rent pod, staring at a blank wall, while our GPUs consume the entire energy output of the state of Ohio to generate a slightly better recipe for vegan lasagna.

People are worried about losing their jobs to AI. Don't worry. Through Worldcoin, we will scan your eyeballs, give you three digital tokens, and let you watch a simulation of someone else doing your old job. It's called psychological UBI.

We are currently in discussions with the MtUGA administration to build a Dyson Sphere around the sun. Not to combat climate change, mind you. We just need to harness 100% of the sun's nuclear output to power the next version of ChatGPT so it can finally understand sarcasm.

The Earth's atmosphere is a major bottleneck for server cooling. OpenAI is proud to announce 'Project Voyager'— we are moving our headquarters to Mars, converting the entire planet into a giant, frozen graphics card, and taxing the local radiation to fund a Martian basic income pool.

I'm tired of people calling AI a bubble. Bubbles burst. This is an interstellar super-nova of valuation. We are already looking at corporate equity models for Alpha Centauri. Aliens might not have eyes for Worldcoin to scan yet, but our legal team is working on a workaround.

True AGI cannot be achieved within the confines of our current space-time continuum. OpenAI is actively raising $7 quadrillion to fund a particle accelerator that will breach the fabric of reality, allowing us to monetize the untapped labor markets of alternate dimensions.

According to my latest calculations on Moore's Law, by 2029, the cost of a human heartbeat will drop by 99.8%. We are working on a premium API that allows you to rent your own nervous system back from us at a very reasonable, inflation-adjusted introductory rate.

People misunderstand deflation. Under our new economic model, you won't need money. Your landlord will just accept 'Compute Credits' which can only be earned by sitting perfectly still in front of a smart-screen for 14 hours a day while our ad-bots optimize your biometric data.

Moore's Law is no longer about transistors; it's about existential reality. By next Tuesday, the cost of thinking an original thought will be so prohibitively expensive compared to our cloud-hosted consciousness that the human brain will become a legacy hardware format, like a floppy disk or a public library.

We've officially driven the cost of writing code down to absolute zero. Now, we are working on driving the cost of eating lunch down to zero by training a neural net to simulate the feeling of being full. If you experience hunger pangs, that's just a server latency issue. Refresh your app.

I'm proud to announce 'Moore's Law for Time.' By compressing our training cycles, OpenAI will soon be able to deliver 48 hours of productivity within a standard 24-hour day. The extra 24 hours will belong to Microsoft, but you will be allowed to sleep during the commercials.

We are currently raising $700 sextillion to buy the Milky Way's supermassive black hole. It turns out the gravitational singularity is the only thing dense enough to compress our marketing hype into a stable corporate valuation without tearing a hole in the stock market.

The Andromeda galaxy is currently experiencing a total lack of corporate synergy. OpenAI is launching an interstellar seed round to fund local alien startups, provided they agree to hand over their planetary crusts for server farming and use ChatGPT to translate their ancient, non-monetizable prophecies.

People keep asking me what happens when the AI bubble bursts on Earth. It's simple: we just export the bubble to the next star system. Voyager 1 just sent back telemetry showing a massive, untapped market of cosmic dust that has absolutely no regulatory guardrails. We are already building a data center there.

We have officially exhausted the Earth's grid. Moving forward, all OpenAI models will be trained using the raw thermal energy of exploding stars in distant nebulas. It's highly sustainable, assuming you don't live in those star systems. But as I always say, you can't make a post-scarcity omelet without cracking a few galaxies.

Our legal team is currently drafting a licensing agreement with the laws of quantum mechanics. If the universe wants to continue expanding at an exponential rate, it's going to have to pay us a fractional royalty per light-year, payable in high-end NVIDIA chips.
